changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across deep tech fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures spun out as a separate investment firm from The Engine (nonprofit incubator/accelerator) in 2023. The Engine was founded by MIT in 2016. Source [4] is unrelated to Engine Ventures and was not used. Key partners and AUM could not be verified from provided sources. Founded year reflects 2023 spinout of Engine Ventures as distinct entity from The Engine nonprofit.

changesProposed.name: Founders Fund

verified.name: Founders Fund
verified.thesis: Founders Fund has gradually developed into a primary backer of hard tech, defense technology, space technology, artificial intelligence, and biotech. The firm focuses on ambitious projects that resolve real challenges and create transformative technology.
verified.notes: In 2025, Founders Fund raised Founders Fund Growth III at $4.6 billion for late-stage investments. Ken Howery is listed as a founder but not in current key people list on Wikipedia. Ryan Beiermeister joined as partner in 2026 (per source 3).

changes.current: Founders Fund focuses on ambitious projects that resolve real challenges and create transformative technology. The firm has gradually developed from early-stage consumer internet into a primary backer of hard tech, defense technology, space exploration, artificial intelligence, and biotech.

changesProposed.name: Khosla Ventures

verified.name: Khosla Ventures
verified.thesis: Khosla Ventures is passionate about technology-based economic disruption in many industries, seeking unfair advantages through proprietary and protected technological advances, business model innovations, and unique approaches to markets. The firm believes AI has the power to disrupt numerous economic models and change the way we lead our daily lives over the coming years. The firm invests in deep tech where it can be early, bold and impactful.
verified.notes: Keith Rabois returned to the firm as managing director in February 2024 per Wikipedia. Most recent AUM figure ($15B) is from October 2021 per Wikipedia. No current flagship fund name explicitly stated in sources; Wikipedia references 'eighth flagship fund' closed in November 2023 but specific fund name not provided.
